get.description:
Return reference data for admin UI sections.

Returns all available permissions, OAuth provider types, and
authentication method metadata. Requires authentication but no
specific permission since this is read-only metadata.

List all API keys for the authenticated user.

Returns metadata for all API keys (active and revoked). The
key secrets are not included in the response.

Args:
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    List of API key metadata

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Api Keys Get

post.description:
Create a new API key for the authenticated user.

The API key secret is returned only once during creation.
Store it securely as it cannot be retrieved later. The key
format is: ik_<16chars>_<32chars>

Args:
    key_request: API key creation parameters
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    APIKeyCreateResponse with key_secret (shown only once)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if expiration exceeds maximum allowed

delete.description:
Revoke an API key (soft delete).

Revoked keys can no longer be used for authentication but
remain in the database for audit purposes.

Args:
    key_id: API key identifier to revoke
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if key not found or not owned by user

post.description:
Rotate an API key secret (keep same key_id, generate new
secret).

This allows updating the key secret without changing the
key_id or other metadata. The old secret is immediately
invalidated and the new secret is returned (shown only once).

Args:
    key_id: API key identifier to rotate
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    APIKeyCreateResponse with new key_secret (shown only once)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if key not found or not owned by user
    HTTPException: 400 if key is already revoked

get.description:
Get available authentication providers configuration.

Returns a list of enabled authentication providers to allow the UI
to dynamically configure the login interface.

Returns:
    AuthProvidersResponse: List of providers with configuration

post.description:
OAuth2 token endpoint for client credentials grant.

Accepts form-encoded parameters per RFC 6749.

Args:
    grant_type: Must be 'client_credentials'
    client_id: Client credential ID (cc_...)
    client_secret: Client secret
    scope: Optional space-separated scopes

Returns:
    OAuth2TokenResponse with access and refresh tokens

Login with email and password (Phase 5: with optional MFA).

Phase 5 adds MFA support. If the user has MFA enabled, they must
provide a valid TOTP code or backup code via the mfa_code parameter.

Args:
    email: User email address
    password: User password
    mfa_code: Optional MFA code (6-digit TOTP or backup code)

Returns:
    JWT tokens (access and refresh)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if credentials are invalid or MFA code
        required. Returns X-MFA-Required: true header if MFA
        is required.

Refresh access token and rotate refresh token (Phase 5).

Phase 5 implements token rotation: the old refresh token is
revoked and a new refresh token is issued alongside the new
access token. This prevents refresh token reuse attacks.

Args:
    refresh_request: Refresh token
    request: FastAPI request object

Returns:
    New JWT access token and NEW refresh token (rotated)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if refresh token is invalid or revoked

Logout and revoke current token and session (Phase 5).

Args:
    auth: Current authenticated user context
    revoke_all_sessions: If True, revoke all user tokens and
        delete all sessions. If False, revoke only current
        token and associated refresh token.

get.description:
Initiate OAuth login flow.

Args:
    provider: OAuth provider ('google', 'github', 'oidc')
    redirect_uri: Where to redirect after successful auth

Returns:
    Redirect to OAuth provider's authorization page

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if provider not enabled or invalid

Handle OAuth provider callback.

After user authorizes on OAuth provider, they're redirected
here with an authorization code. We exchange it for tokens
and create/login user.

Args:
    provider: OAuth provider
    code: Authorization code from provider
    state: State parameter for CSRF protection
    error: Error code if auth failed
    error_description: Human-readable error description

Returns:
    Redirect to frontend with token or error

post.description: Create a new blueprint node in the graph database.

get.description: Retrieve all blueprints, optionally filtered.
Retrieve blueprints filtered by node type or relationship.

schema.title: Response List Blueprints By Type Blueprints  Type  Get

get.description: Retrieve a blueprint by type (or 'relationship') and slug.

patch.description:
Partially update a blueprint using JSON Patch (RFC 6902).

Parameters:
    blueprint_type: Blueprint type from URL (or 'relationship').
    slug: Blueprint slug from URL.
    operations: JSON Patch operations.

Returns:
    The updated blueprint.

Raises:
    400: Invalid patch, read-only path, or slug/type mismatch.
    404: Blueprint not found.
    422: Patch test failed or validation error.

delete.description: Delete a blueprint by type (or 'relationship') and slug.

post.description:
Create a new client credential for a service account.

The client secret is returned only once during creation. Store it
securely as it cannot be retrieved later.

Args:
    slug: Service account slug identifier
    credential_request: Client credential creation parameters
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    ClientCredentialCreateResponse with client_secret
    (shown once)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if service account not found
    HTTPException: 400 if expiration exceeds maximum allowed

get.description:
List all client credentials for a service account.

Returns metadata for all credentials (active and revoked).
Client secrets are not included in the response.

Args:
    slug: Service account slug identifier
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    List of client credential metadata

schema.title: Response List Client Credentials Service Accounts  Slug  Client Credentials Get

delete.description:
Revoke a client credential (soft delete).

Revoked credentials can no longer be used for authentication
but remain in the database for audit purposes.

Args:
    slug: Service account slug identifier
    client_id: Client credential identifier to revoke
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if credential not found or not owned
        by the service account

post.description:
Rotate a client credential secret.

Generates a new secret while keeping the same client_id and
metadata. The old secret is immediately invalidated and the
new secret is returned (shown only once).

Args:
    slug: Service account slug identifier
    client_id: Client credential identifier to rotate
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    ClientCredentialCreateResponse with new client_secret

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if credential not found or not owned
        by the service account
    HTTPException: 400 if credential is already revoked

get.description:
Get MFA status for the authenticated user.

Args:
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    MFA status including enabled flag and backup codes remaining

post.description:
Setup MFA for the authenticated user (not enabled until
verified).

Generates a TOTP secret, QR code, and backup codes. MFA is not
enabled until the user verifies a code using the /mfa/verify
endpoint.

Args:
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    MFA setup data including secret, QR code, and backup codes
    (shown only once)

Verify TOTP code and enable MFA for the authenticated user.

This endpoint must be called after /mfa/setup to enable MFA.
The user must provide a valid TOTP code from their authenticator
app to prove they have successfully configured it.

Args:
    verify_request: TOTP code to verify
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if MFA not setup, 401 if code is invalid

delete.description:
Disable MFA for the authenticated user.

Disabling MFA requires identity verification:
- For password-based users: provide current_password
- For OAuth-only users: provide mfa_code (TOTP or backup code)

This permanently deletes the TOTP secret and backup codes.

Args:
    auth: Current authenticated user context
    current_password: User's current password (for
        password-based users)
    mfa_code: MFA code (for OAuth-only users without password)

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if verification fails or neither
        credential provided

get.description: List operations log entries (newest first, keyset paginated).

patch.description: Apply a JSON Patch to an operations log entry.

delete.description: Soft-delete an operations log entry (tombstone insert).

get.description:
List every DEPENDS_ON edge touching the project.

Returns both inbound and outbound edges in a flat list with a
``direction`` field. Rows are sorted inbound first, then by
the related project's name.

put.description:
Replace the outbound DEPENDS_ON edges for a project.

Deletes all existing outbound DEPENDS_ON edges and creates new
ones for each project ID in ``depends_on``.  Self-references
are silently ignored.

The API key secret is returned only once during creation.

format is: ik_<16chars>_<32chars>

    key_request: API key creation parameters
    auth: Current authenticated user context

Returns:
    APIKeyCreateResponse with key_secret (shown only once)

Returns:

Raises:

        service account
    HTTPException: 400 if key is already revoked

post.description:
Upload a file.

Accepts a multipart file upload, validates the content type and
size, stores the file in S3, generates a thumbnail for raster
images, and creates an Upload node in the graph.

Returns:
    Upload metadata including the generated ID.

Raises:
    400: If the file fails validation.
    401: Not authenticated.
    403: Missing ``upload:create`` permission.

get.description:
List uploads with optional filters.

Parameters:
    content_type: Filter by MIME type.
    uploaded_by: Filter by uploader email.

Returns:
    List of upload metadata records.

Serve the uploaded file.

Pulls the file through from S3 and serves it directly.

Returns:
    The file content with appropriate content type.

Raises:
    404: If the upload does not exist.

delete.description:
Delete an upload and its S3 objects.

Removes the graph node and deletes the original file and
thumbnail (if present) from S3.

Raises:
    404: If the upload does not exist.

get.description:
Return upload metadata as JSON.

Raises:
    404: If the upload does not exist.

Serve the upload thumbnail.

Pulls the thumbnail through from S3 and serves it directly.

Returns:
    The thumbnail image as image/webp.

Raises:
    404: If the upload does not exist or has no thumbnail.

post.description:
Create a new user account.

Parameters:
    user_create (models.UserCreate): User creation data including
        optional password. If password is None, creates an OAuth-only
        user.

Returns:
    models.UserResponse: The created user (without password_hash).

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 409 if email already exists.

get.description:
Retrieve all users with optional filtering.

Parameters:
    is_active (bool | None): If provided, filter by active status.
    is_admin (bool | None): If provided, filter by admin status.

Returns:
    list[models.UserResponse]: Users ordered by email, without
        password hashes.

Retrieve a user by email with loaded organization memberships.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email address of the user to retrieve.

Returns:
    models.UserResponse: User with loaded organizations, without
        password hash.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 404 if user not found.

put.description:
Update an existing user account.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email from URL path.
    user_update (models.UserCreate): Updated user data.

Returns:
    models.UserResponse: The updated user (without password_hash).

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 400 if URL email doesn't match
        body email, or HTTP 404 if user not found.

patch.description:
Partially update a user using JSON Patch (RFC 6902).

Parameters:
    email: Email from URL path (percent-encoded).
    operations: JSON Patch operations.

Returns:
    The updated user.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 400 if invalid patch, read-only
        path, or business logic violation. HTTP 403 if insufficient
        privileges. HTTP 404 if user not found. HTTP 422 if patch
        test failed.

delete.description:
Delete a user account.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email of user to delete.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 400 if trying to delete yourself,
        or HTTP 404 if user not found.

post.description:
Change a user's password.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email of user whose password to change.
    password_change (models.PasswordChangeRequest): Password change
        data with current and new passwords.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 401 if current password is wrong
        (non-admin), HTTP 403 if not self or admin, or HTTP 404
        if user not found.

Add a user to an organization with a role.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email of user to add.
    membership (dict): Dictionary with 'organization_slug' and
        'role_slug' keys.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 400 if required fields missing,
        HTTP 404 if user, organization, or role not found.

put.description:
Change a user's role in an organization.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email of user.
    org_slug (str): Organization slug.
    role_data (dict): Dictionary with 'role_slug' key.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 400 if role_slug missing,
        HTTP 404 if membership or role not found.

delete.description:
Remove a user from an organization.

Parameters:
    email (str): Email of user.
    org_slug (str): Organization slug.

Raises:
    fastapi.HTTPException: HTTP 404 if membership not found.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable name for the API key

APIKeyCreate.title: APIKeyCreate
APIKeyCreate.description: Request model for creating an API key.

key_secret.title: Key Secret
key_secret.description: Full API key secret (shown only once)

APIKeyCreateResponse.title: APIKeyCreateResponse
APIKeyCreateResponse.description:
Response model for API key creation (includes secret,
shown once).

APIKeyResponse.title: APIKeyResponse
APIKeyResponse.description: Response model for API key metadata (without secret).

AdminSettings.title: AdminSettings
AdminSettings.description: Reference data for the admin UI.

BlueprintFilter.title: BlueprintFilter
BlueprintFilter.description:
Filter criteria for blueprint applicability.

All fields use ``list[str]`` — a blueprint matches when the
context value is contained in the list.  Multiple fields are
ANDed together.  Omitted fields match everything.

BlueprintSection.title: BlueprintSection
BlueprintSection.description: One blueprint's contribution to the project schema.

BlueprintSectionProperty.title: BlueprintSectionProperty
BlueprintSectionProperty.description: A single property from a blueprint's JSON Schema.

ClientCredentialCreate.title: ClientCredentialCreate
ClientCredentialCreate.description: Request model for creating client credentials.

ClientCredentialCreateResponse.title: ClientCredentialCreateResponse
ClientCredentialCreateResponse.description: Response model for client credential creation (secret shown once).

ClientCredentialResponse.title: ClientCredentialResponse
ClientCredentialResponse.description: Response model for client credentials (without secret).

DeploymentEvent.title: DeploymentEvent
DeploymentEvent.description:
A single status transition for a release deployment.

A release accumulates a list of these on the ``DEPLOYED_TO``
edge to a given ``Environment`` — one per status transition.

DeploymentEventInput.title: DeploymentEventInput
DeploymentEventInput.description: Request body for recording a deployment event.

EnvironmentRef.title: EnvironmentRef
EnvironmentRef.description:
Environment with dynamic edge properties from DEPLOYED_IN.

Edge properties are defined by relationship blueprints and
accepted via ``extra='allow'`` so they flow through to the
response without hard-coding field names.

ExistsInCreate.title: ExistsInCreate
ExistsInCreate.description: Request model for creating a Project EXISTS_IN link.

ExistsInResponse.title: ExistsInResponse
ExistsInResponse.description: Response model for a Project EXISTS_IN link.

FormatType.title: FormatType
FormatType.description: JSON Schema format enumeration for string validation.

LinkDefinitionCreate.title: LinkDefinitionCreate
LinkDefinitionCreate.description: Request model for creating a link definition.

LinkDefinitionUpdate.title: LinkDefinitionUpdate
LinkDefinitionUpdate.description: Request model for updating a link definition.

provisioning_uri.title: Provisioning Uri
provisioning_uri.description: TOTP provisioning URI for authenticator apps

backup_codes.title: Backup Codes
backup_codes.description: One-time backup codes for account recovery

MFASetupResponse.title: MFASetupResponse
MFASetupResponse.description: Response model for MFA setup (includes secret and QR code).

MFAVerifyRequest.title: MFAVerifyRequest
MFAVerifyRequest.description: Request model for MFA code verification.

OperationLogResponse.title: OperationLogResponse
OperationLogResponse.description: Public projection of an OperationLog entry.

OrgMembership.title: OrgMembership
OrgMembership.description: Organization membership with role for API responses.

PasswordChangeRequest.title: PasswordChangeRequest
PasswordChangeRequest.description: Request model for changing user passwords.

PatchOperation.title: PatchOperation
PatchOperation.description:
A single JSON Patch operation (RFC 6902).

Attributes:
    op: The operation type.
    path: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) target path.
    value: New value for add/replace/test operations.
    from_: Source path for move/copy operations.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: Permission for a specific resource action.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Map of environment slug to edge properties. Example: {"production": {"url": "https://..."}, "staging": {}}

ProjectCreate.title: ProjectCreate
ProjectCreate.description:
Request body for creating a project.

Blueprint-defined fields are accepted as extra properties.

ProjectRelationship.title: ProjectRelationship
ProjectRelationship.description: A single DEPENDS_ON edge touching the project.

ProjectRelationshipSummary.title: ProjectRelationshipSummary
ProjectRelationshipSummary.description: Summary of the project on the other end of an edge.

ProjectRelationships.title: ProjectRelationships
ProjectRelationships.description: Typed relationship links and counts for a project.

depends_on.title: Depends On
depends_on.description: Project IDs that this project depends on.

ProjectRelationshipsUpdate.title: ProjectRelationshipsUpdate
ProjectRelationshipsUpdate.description: Request body for replacing outbound DEPENDS_ON edges.

ProjectSchemaResponse.title: ProjectSchemaResponse
ProjectSchemaResponse.description: Fully resolved, blueprint-grouped schema for a project.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Map of environment slug to edge properties. Replaces all environment assignments when provided.

ProjectUpdate.title: ProjectUpdate
ProjectUpdate.description:
Request body for updating a project.

Blueprint-defined fields are accepted as extra properties.

RelationshipLink.title: RelationshipLink
RelationshipLink.description: A hypermedia-style link to related resources.

ReleaseCreate.title: ReleaseCreate
ReleaseCreate.description: Request body for creating a release.

ReleaseEnvironmentEdgeResponse.title: ReleaseEnvironmentEdgeResponse
ReleaseEnvironmentEdgeResponse.description: Response body for a release/environment deployment edge.

ReleaseEnvironmentRef.title: ReleaseEnvironmentRef
ReleaseEnvironmentRef.description: Minimal environment identity returned on edge responses.

ReleaseLink.title: ReleaseLink
ReleaseLink.description:
A typed external link attached to a ``Release``.

``type`` is a free-form discriminator (e.g. ``github_release``
or ``jira_version``); ``label`` is an optional display string.

ServiceAccountCreate.title: ServiceAccountCreate
ServiceAccountCreate.description: Request model for creating service accounts.

ServiceAccountUpdate.title: ServiceAccountUpdate
ServiceAccountUpdate.description: Request model for updating service accounts.

ServiceApplicationCreate.title: ServiceApplicationCreate
ServiceApplicationCreate.description: Request model for creating a service application.

ServiceApplicationResponse.title: ServiceApplicationResponse
ServiceApplicationResponse.description: Response model for service applications (no secrets).

ServiceApplicationSecretsUpdate.title: ServiceApplicationSecretsUpdate
ServiceApplicationSecretsUpdate.description: Request model for updating application secrets.

ServiceApplicationUpdate.title: ServiceApplicationUpdate
ServiceApplicationUpdate.description: Request model for updating non-secret application fields.

ThirdPartyServiceCreate.title: ThirdPartyServiceCreate
ThirdPartyServiceCreate.description: Request model for creating a third-party service.

ThirdPartyServiceResponse.title: ThirdPartyServiceResponse
ThirdPartyServiceResponse.description: Response model for a third-party service.

ThirdPartyServiceUpdate.title: ThirdPartyServiceUpdate
ThirdPartyServiceUpdate.description:
Request model for updating a third-party service.

Designed for GET-modify-PUT: the client fetches the full resource,
modifies fields, and sends the complete object back. All fields
that exist on the response are required (no silent defaults).

TokenRefreshRequest.title: TokenRefreshRequest
TokenRefreshRequest.description: Request to refresh an access token.

notification_path.title: Notification Path
notification_path.description: URL path for receiving webhooks (must start with /)

identifier_selector.title: Identifier Selector
identifier_selector.description: JSON Path expression to extract project identifier

WebhookCreate.title: WebhookCreate
WebhookCreate.description: Request model for creating a webhook.

handler.title: Handler
handler.description: Python callable in dotted import syntax

handler_config.title: Handler Config
handler_config.description: Structured configuration passed to the handler

WebhookRuleCreate.description: A single rule within a webhook (no ordinal — position is implicit).

WebhookRuleResponse.title: WebhookRuleResponse
WebhookRuleResponse.description: Rule in a webhook response (no ordinal exposed).

identifier_selector.title: Identifier Selector
identifier_selector.description: JSON Path expression to extract project identifier

WebhookUpdate.title: WebhookUpdate
WebhookUpdate.description: Request model for updating a webhook (GET-modify-PUT).

url.description: The deployment URL for this project in this environment
